Clan Mercante-Diomede.description: Italian criminal organization based in Bari, founded by a confederation of the Mercante and Diomede families. Active in drug trafficking and other illicit activities in several Bari neighborhoods. Known for the 'Strage di San Valentino' in 2000, a failed attack on a rival clan. The organization has been involved in violent turf wars.

'Ndrangheta.description: Italian criminal organization of mafia type originating from Calabria. Recognized as a unitary criminal organization with a collegial leadership by the Italian Supreme Court in 2016. It is the only mafia present on all five continents, with an estimated global turnover of 150 billion euros as of 2022. Its main activity is drug trafficking, followed by involvement in public contracts, vote buying, extortion, usury, arms trafficking, gambling, and toxic waste disposal.

Yüksekova Gang.description: The Yüksekova Gang was an illegal organization based in the Yüksekova district of Hakkari Province, Turkey, accused of killing 16 people. It had apparent links to the Turkish Gendarmerie's JITEM unit and possible links to the Ergenekon organization. The gang smuggled drugs and weapons, and its activities were first revealed in 1996 after the Susurluk scandal. Legal cases against the gang were sent to the European Court of Human Rights.

'Ndrina Torcasio.description: The 'Ndrina Torcasio is a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, a Calabrian mafia organization, based in Lamezia Terme, Italy. It is allied with the Gualtieri, Cerra, and Giorgi-Pizzata clans. The clan has been involved in a long-running feud (the Faida di Lamezia Terme) with the Iannazzo-Giampà clan since 2000, resulting in dozens of murders. In 2015, a peace mediated by Reggio Calabria clans was revealed through Operation Andromeda.

Colacurcio Organization.description: The Colacurcio Organization was a criminal group based in Seattle, Washington, founded in the 1950s by Frank Colacurcio. It operated strip clubs and vending machines as fronts for racketeering, prostitution, and money laundering. The organization was dismantled after a series of indictments in 2009-2010, culminating in Colacurcio Sr.'s death in 2010 and his son's conviction.

descriptions: Colacurcio met with a Bonanno family member to discuss a business relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1970s, Colacurcio met with Bonanno crime family member Salvatore "Bill" Bonanno in Yakima, Washington to discuss a business relationship.

Bounty Hunter Watts Bloods.description: The Bounty Hunter Watts Bloods is a set of the Bloods gang alliance based in the Nickerson Gardens public housing projects in Watts, Los Angeles. Founded in 1969 by Gary Barker and Bobby Jack, it is known for its violent rivalry with the Grape Street Watts Crips. The gang has over 2,000 documented members and is involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and murder. A 2004 injunction limited members' movements.

Hopi Boys.description: The Hopi Boys are an Afro-Curaçaoan criminal group based in the south-east region of Amsterdam, Netherlands. They are one of the most influential gangs in the Dutch capital, controlling rackets, drug dealing, and prostitution. The group is predominantly Afro-Curaçaoan and has allies such as the Green gang and rivals like the Kloekhorststraat Gang.

Ortiz gang.description: An illegalist anarchist group active in Western Europe in the early 1890s, composed of French and Italian militants. The gang committed a series of burglaries and played a central role in the Trial of the Thirty. Its members were convicted, leading to the group's dissolution.

descriptions: The Ortiz Gang followed and was influenced by the Intransigents of London and Paris.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang is known for its series of burglaries, its influence on the emergence and development of illegalism, following Vittorio Pini's Intransigents of London and Paris

Aryan Circle.description: The Aryan Circle is a white supremacist neo-Nazi prison gang operating in the United States, founded in 1985 by Mark 'Cowboy' Gaspard in the Texas Department of Corrections as a splinter group from the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas. It is the second-largest prison gang in Texas, with members involved in drug trafficking, murder, and extortion. The gang remains active in state and federal prisons.

The Yogurt Connection.description: A drug smuggling ring based in Indianapolis, Indiana, operating in the late 1970s and early 1980s. Organized by Linda Leary and her sons Richard and Paul Heilbrunn, it smuggled an estimated 250,000 pounds of marijuana from Colombia, Jamaica, and Thailand into eleven midwestern states, valued at $50-100 million. The ring used a health-food business as a front and laundered money through offshore accounts.

Chapare Cartel.description: Bolivian criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and human trafficking, operating in the Chapare region. It emerged from the merger of six coca growers' federations in the Chapare region, with origins dating back to 1964. The cartel is alleged to have strong connections with the Movement for Socialism political party and former president Evo Morales. Its current structure is unknown.

Matsuba-kai.description: The Matsuba-kai is a yakuza organization based in Tokyo, Japan, founded in March 1953 from remnants of the Sekine-gumi and Fujita-gumi. It is a designated yakuza group with an estimated 280 active members as of 2024. The group is a member of the Kantō Hatsuka-kai federation and has been involved in drug smuggling, violent feuds, and protests.
